channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Twist-angle tunable Josephson junctions in three-dimensional superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Tenta Tani, Takuto Kawakami, and Mikito Koshino

We theoretically investigate the superconducting phase and perpendicular Josephson supercurrent in twisted three-dimensional (3D) superconductors, where two layered 3D materials are stacked with a relative twist. We formulate the Bogoliubov–de Gennes Hamiltonian and develop a self-consistent method …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035418] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tenta Tani, Takuto Kawakami, and Mikito Koshino

We theoretically investigate the superconducting phase and perpendicular Josephson supercurrent in twisted three-dimensional (3D) superconductors, where two layered 3D materials are stacked with a relative twist. We formulate the Bogoliubov–de Gennes Hamiltonian and develop a self-consistent method …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035418] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Twist-angle tunable Josephson junctions in three-dimensional superconductors
item.dc:creator: Tenta Tani, Takuto Kawakami, and Mikito Koshino

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Interplay of unidirectional quantum strings in a kagome Rydberg atom array
item.description:
Author(s): Wei Xu and Xue-Feng Zhang

Leveraging the rapid development of quantum simulators, the intriguing phenomena of quantum strings are observed across various quantum simulation platforms. However, the complex interplay between the quantum strings cannot be well analyzed due to the limited system size in real quantum simulators. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020408] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wei Xu and Xue-Feng Zhang

Leveraging the rapid development of quantum simulators, the intriguing phenomena of quantum strings are observed across various quantum simulation platforms. However, the complex interplay between the quantum strings cannot be well analyzed due to the limited system size in real quantum simulators. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020408] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Interplay of unidirectional quantum strings in a kagome Rydberg atom array
item.dc:creator: Wei Xu and Xue-Feng Zhang

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Dominant orbital magnetization in the prototypical altermagnet MnTe
item.description:
Author(s): Chao Chen Ye, Karma Tenzin, Jagoda Sławińska, and Carmine Autieri

Altermagnetism is an unconventional form of antiferromagnetism characterized by momentum-dependent spin polarization of electronic states and zero net magnetization, arising from specific crystalline symmetries. In the presence of spin-orbit coupling (SOC), altermagnets can exhibit finite net magnet…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014413] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chao Chen Ye, Karma Tenzin, Jagoda Sławińska, and Carmine Autieri

Altermagnetism is an unconventional form of antiferromagnetism characterized by momentum-dependent spin polarization of electronic states and zero net magnetization, arising from specific crystalline symmetries. In the presence of spin-orbit coupling (SOC), altermagnets can exhibit finite net magnet…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014413] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Dominant orbital magnetization in the prototypical altermagnet MnTe
item.dc:creator: Chao Chen Ye, Karma Tenzin, Jagoda Sławińska, and Carmine Autieri
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Layer-resolved flat-band excitations in magic-angle twisted bilayer phononic graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Chenzhe Wang, Jiawei Ruan, Steven R. Craig, Zhenglu Li, Yan Deng, Steven G. Louie, and Chengzhi Shi

Although rich phenomena have been microscopically observed in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene, layer-resolved excitations within the flat bands, which provide a direct understanding of the coupling between the two layers with subtle interlayer interactions, have not been directly observed. Such…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024306] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chenzhe Wang, Jiawei Ruan, Steven R. Craig, Zhenglu Li, Yan Deng, Steven G. Louie, and Chengzhi Shi

Although rich phenomena have been microscopically observed in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene, layer-resolved excitations within the flat bands, which provide a direct understanding of the coupling between the two layers with subtle interlayer interactions, have not been directly observed. Such…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024306] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Layer-resolved flat-band excitations in magic-angle twisted bilayer phononic graphene
item.dc:creator: Chenzhe Wang, Jiawei Ruan, Steven R. Craig, Zhenglu Li, Yan Deng, Steven G. Louie, and Chengzhi Shi

item.dc:subject: Dynamics, dynamical systems, lattice effects

item.title: Superconductivity, Kondo physics, and magnetic order: Tuning the ground state in the ${\mathrm{La}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Ce}}_{x}\mathrm{FeSiH}$ solid solution through the interplay between $3d$ and $4f$ correlated electrons
item.description:
Author(s): J. Sourd, B. Vignolle, E. Gaudin, S. Burdin, and S. Tencé

We report a study of the ${\mathrm{La}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Ce}}_{x}\mathrm{FeSiH}$ solid solution $(0≤x≤1)$, a family of intermetallic hydrides of ZrCuSiAs-type structure, with space group $P4/nmm$. For low cerium concentrations $x≤0.20$, we observe the presence of superconductivity, which originates fro…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035120] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. Sourd, B. Vignolle, E. Gaudin, S. Burdin, and S. Tencé

We report a study of the La1−xCexFeSiH solid solution (0≤x≤1), a family of intermetallic hydrides of ZrCuSiAs-type structure, with space group P4/nmm. For low cerium concentrations x≤0.20, we observe the presence of superconductivity, which originates from the correlated 3d electrons of iron. The su…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035120] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Superconductivity, Kondo physics, and magnetic order: Tuning the ground state in the ${\mathrm{La}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Ce}}_{x}\mathrm{FeSiH}$ solid solution through the interplay between $3d$ and $4f$ correlated electrons
item.dc:creator: J. Sourd, B. Vignolle, E. Gaudin, S. Burdin, and S. Tencé

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Hilbert space fragmentation in hardcore Bose and Fermi Hubbard models on generalized Lieb lattices
item.description:
Author(s): D. K. He and Z. Song

We study the Hilbert space fragmentation (HSF) in hardcore Bose and Fermi Hubbard models in the framework of the restricted spectrum generating algebra (RSGA). We present a family of hardcore Bose-Hubbard models with repulsive density-density interactions on a generalized Lieb lattice. We show that …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035123] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): D. K. He and Z. Song

We study the Hilbert space fragmentation (HSF) in hardcore Bose and Fermi Hubbard models in the framework of the restricted spectrum generating algebra (RSGA). We present a family of hardcore Bose-Hubbard models with repulsive density-density interactions on a generalized Lieb lattice. We show that …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035123] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Hilbert space fragmentation in hardcore Bose and Fermi Hubbard models on generalized Lieb lattices
item.dc:creator: D. K. He and Z. Song

item.title: Magnetism in the single-atom-thick ${\mathrm{EuPb}}_{3}$ kagome compound on Si(111) studied using in situ transport and magnetotransport measurements
item.description:
Author(s): N. V. Denisov, L. V. Bondarenko, A. Y. Tupchaya, Y. E. Vekovshinin, V. G. Kotlyar, T. V. Utas, P. V. Burkovskaya, L. O. Brykin, A. N. Mihalyuk, S. V. Eremeev, D. V. Gruznev, A. V. Zotov, and A. A. Saranin

Transport and magnetotransport properties of the single-atom-thick ${\mathrm{EuPb}}_{3}$ kagome compound on Si(111) were explored in situ using the linear four-point-probe technique within a temperature range from 2.5 to 40 K and at the magnetic field varied from $−6$ to $+6\phantom{\rule{0.16em}{0e…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035415] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): N. V. Denisov, L. V. Bondarenko, A. Y. Tupchaya, Y. E. Vekovshinin, V. G. Kotlyar, T. V. Utas, P. V. Burkovskaya, L. O. Brykin, A. N. Mihalyuk, S. V. Eremeev, D. V. Gruznev, A. V. Zotov, and A. A. Saranin

Transport and magnetotransport properties of the single-atom-thick EuPb3 kagome compound on Si(111) were explored in situ using the linear four-point-probe technique within a temperature range from 2.5 to 40 K and at the magnetic field varied from −6 to +6T. Our results revealed that EuPb3 displays …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035415] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetism in the single-atom-thick ${\mathrm{EuPb}}_{3}$ kagome compound on Si(111) studied using in situ transport and magnetotransport measurements

item.title: Quantum critical dynamical response of the twisted Kitaev spin chain
item.description:
Author(s): Uliana E. Khodaeva, Dmitry L. Kovrizhin, and Johannes Knolle

The dynamical structure factor of the transverse field Ising model (TFIM) shows universal power-law divergence at its quantum critical point, signatures of which have been arguably observed in inelastic neutron scattering studies of quantum spin chain materials, for example ${\mathrm{CoNb}}_{2}{\mat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045120] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Uliana E. Khodaeva, Dmitry L. Kovrizhin, and Johannes Knolle

The dynamical structure factor of the transverse field Ising model (TFIM) shows universal power-law divergence at its quantum critical point, signatures of which have been arguably observed in inelastic neutron scattering studies of quantum spin chain materials, for example CoNb2O6. However, it has …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045120] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum critical dynamical response of the twisted Kitaev spin chain
item.dc:creator: Uliana E. Khodaeva, Dmitry L. Kovrizhin, and Johannes Knolle

item.title: Physical properties and correlated electron behavior of the kagome metal $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{u}}_{3}{\mathrm{V}}_{2}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{b}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): Mengqin Liu, Chuanlin Liu, Hanlin Wu, Xin Zhou, Dan Liu, Sheng Li, and Zhixiang Shi

We report the successful synthesis and comprehensive characterization of the kagome metal $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{u}}_{3}{\mathrm{V}}_{2}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{b}}_{4}$. This material exhibits a quadratic temperature dependence of resistivity over a broad temperature range, indicative of strong electron-ele…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045123] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mengqin Liu, Chuanlin Liu, Hanlin Wu, Xin Zhou, Dan Liu, Sheng Li, and Zhixiang Shi

We report the successful synthesis and comprehensive characterization of the kagome metal Cu3V2Sb4. This material exhibits a quadratic temperature dependence of resistivity over a broad temperature range, indicative of strong electron-electron interactions, as confirmed by the fact that specific hea…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045123] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Physical properties and correlated electron behavior of the kagome metal $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{u}}_{3}{\mathrm{V}}_{2}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{b}}_{4}$

item.title: Interconvertibility in the thermodynamic properties of Lieb-kagome lattices
item.description:
Author(s): T. F. O. Lara, E. B. Barros, M. Godoy, R. N. Costa Filho, T. A. S. Pereira, and D. R. da Costa

The evolution of thermodynamic properties through the interconvertibility process between Lieb and kagome lattices is explored in the presence of a magnetic field and intrinsic spin-orbit coupling (ISOC). Both lattices share similar crystallographic characteristics that allow for description by a ge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045413] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): T. F. O. Lara, E. B. Barros, M. Godoy, R. N. Costa Filho, T. A. S. Pereira, and D. R. da Costa

The evolution of thermodynamic properties through the interconvertibility process between Lieb and kagome lattices is explored in the presence of a magnetic field and intrinsic spin-orbit coupling (ISOC). Both lattices share similar crystallographic characteristics that allow for description by a ge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045413] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026

item.title: de Haas–van Alphen effect in the Ti-doped ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ kagome compound under magnetic fields up to 41 Tesla
item.description:
Author(s): Kyryl Shtefiienko, Tucker Beekmann, Matthew J. Stitz, Ganesh Pokharel, Stephen D. Wilson, Christopher A. Mizzi, David E. Graf, and Keshav Shrestha

The recently discovered kagome superconductor ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ exhibits a rich interplay between superconductivity (SC) and charge density wave order, both of which are highly tunable via doping or pressure. In particular, hole doping ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ by sub…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035119] Published Fri Jan 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kyryl Shtefiienko, Tucker Beekmann, Matthew J. Stitz, Ganesh Pokharel, Stephen D. Wilson, Christopher A. Mizzi, David E. Graf, and Keshav Shrestha

The recently discovered kagome superconductor CsV3Sb5 exhibits a rich interplay between superconductivity (SC) and charge density wave order, both of which are highly tunable via doping or pressure. In particular, hole doping CsV3Sb5 by substituting vanadium with the relatively smaller titanium allo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035119] Published Fri Jan 09, 2026
item.dc:title: de Haas–van Alphen effect in the Ti-doped ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ kagome compound under magnetic fields up to 41 Tesla
item.dc:creator: Kyryl Shtefiienko, Tucker Beekmann, Matthew J. Stitz, Ganesh Pokharel, Stephen D. Wilson, Christopher A. Mizzi, David E. Graf, and Keshav Shrestha

item.title: Terahertz harmonic generation across the Mott insulator-metal transition
item.description:
Author(s): G. L. Prajapati, S. Ray, I. Ilyakov, A. N. Ponomaryov, A. Arshad, T. V. A. G. de Oliveira, G. Dubey, D. S. Rana, J.-C. Deinert, P. Werner, and S. Kovalev

High harmonic generation (HHG) in the terahertz (THz) regime is an emerging field that has already provided new insights into fundamental low-energy processes in Dirac materials and high-${T}_{\text{C}}$ superconductors. Here, we demonstrate THz harmonic generation across the Mott insulator-metal tr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045118] Published Fri Jan 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): G. L. Prajapati, S. Ray, I. Ilyakov, A. N. Ponomaryov, A. Arshad, T. V. A. G. de Oliveira, G. Dubey, D. S. Rana, J.-C. Deinert, P. Werner, and S. Kovalev

High harmonic generation (HHG) in the terahertz (THz) regime is an emerging field that has already provided new insights into fundamental low-energy processes in Dirac materials and high-TC superconductors. Here, we demonstrate THz harmonic generation across the Mott insulator-metal transition in ra…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045118] Published Fri Jan 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Terahertz harmonic generation across the Mott insulator-metal transition
item.dc:creator: G. L. Prajapati, S. Ray, I. Ilyakov, A. N. Ponomaryov, A. Arshad, T. V. A. G. de Oliveira, G. Dubey, D. S. Rana, J.-C. Deinert, P. Werner, and S. Kovalev

item.title: Decoupled interband pairing in a bilayer iron-based superconductor evidenced by ultrahigh-resolution angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy
item.description:
Author(s): Shichong Wang, Yuanyuan Yang, Yang Li, Wenshan Hong, Huaxun Li, Shaofeng Duan, Lingxiao Gu, Haoran Liu, Jiongyu Huang, Jianzhe Liu, Dong Qian, Guanghan Cao, Huiqian Luo, and Wentao Zhang

We present direct experimental evidence of a weakly coupled multiband superconducting state in the bilayer iron-based superconductor ${\mathrm{ACa}}_{2}{\mathrm{Fe}}_{4}{\mathrm{As}}_{4}{\mathrm{F}}_{2}$ (A = K, Cs) via ultrahigh-resolution angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES). Remarkab…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020502] Published Fri Jan 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shichong Wang, Yuanyuan Yang, Yang Li, Wenshan Hong, Huaxun Li, Shaofeng Duan, Lingxiao Gu, Haoran Liu, Jiongyu Huang, Jianzhe Liu, Dong Qian, Guanghan Cao, Huiqian Luo, and Wentao Zhang

We present direct experimental evidence of a weakly coupled multiband superconducting state in the bilayer iron-based superconductor ACa2Fe4As4F2 (A = K, Cs) via ultrahigh-resolution angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES). Remarkably, the K-containing compound exhibits two distinct transi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020502] Published Fri Jan 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Decoupled interband pairing in a bilayer iron-based superconductor evidenced by ultrahigh-resolution angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy
item.dc:creator: Shichong Wang, Yuanyuan Yang, Yang Li, Wenshan Hong, Huaxun Li, Shaofeng Duan, Lingxiao Gu, Haoran Liu, Jiongyu Huang, Jianzhe Liu, Dong Qian, Guanghan Cao, Huiqian Luo, and Wentao Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Enhanced Kohn-Luttinger superconductivity in geometric bands
item.description:
Author(s): Ammar Jahin and Shi-Zeng Lin

The authors reveal here that quantum geometry, encoded in the Berry curvature and wavefunction structure of electronic bands, can dramatically enhance Kohn–Luttinger superconductivity in two dimensions. They show that electrons in a geometrically nontrivial band can form a high-Tc topological superconductor purely from repulsive interactions, enabled by a resonance between the Cooper pair angular momentum and the Berry flux enclosed by the Fermi surface. These results provide a geometric route to strong superconductivity and offer new insight into rhombohedral graphene and twisted transition metal dichalcogenide systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014504] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ammar Jahin and Shi-Zeng Lin

The authors reveal here that quantum geometry, encoded in the Berry curvature and wavefunction structure of electronic bands, can dramatically enhance Kohn–Luttinger superconductivity in two dimensions. They show that electrons in a geometrically nontrivial band can form a high-Tc topological superconductor purely from repulsive interactions, enabled by a resonance between the Cooper pair angular momentum and the Berry flux enclosed by the Fermi surface. These results provide a geometric route to strong superconductivity and offer new insight into rhombohedral graphene and twisted transition metal dichalcogenide systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014504] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Enhanced Kohn-Luttinger superconductivity in geometric bands
item.dc:creator: Ammar Jahin and Shi-Zeng Lin
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Quantized crossed Andreev reflection in altermagnet/altermagnet topological superconductor/altermagnet heterojunction
item.description:
Author(s): Xing Wang, Kai-Yi Lyu, Yong Guo, and Yu-Xian Li

We propose an altermagnet/altermagnet topological superconductor/altermagnet (AM/AMTSC/AM) heterojunction exhibiting a filtering effect, where the vast majority of incident electrons are locally reflected, and only a small portion passes through the center region of the AMTSC. Within an appropriate …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014505] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xing Wang, Kai-Yi Lyu, Yong Guo, and Yu-Xian Li

We propose an altermagnet/altermagnet topological superconductor/altermagnet (AM/AMTSC/AM) heterojunction exhibiting a filtering effect, where the vast majority of incident electrons are locally reflected, and only a small portion passes through the center region of the AMTSC. Within an appropriate …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014505] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantized crossed Andreev reflection in altermagnet/altermagnet topological superconductor/altermagnet heterojunction
item.dc:creator: Xing Wang, Kai-Yi Lyu, Yong Guo, and Yu-Xian Li

item.title: Phase transitions and critical exponents in the six-vertex model on kagome lattices
item.description:
Author(s): Wei Zhang, Wanzhou Zhang, Jie Zhang, Chengxiang Ding, and Youjin Deng

Inspired by the experimental realization of direct kagome spin ice [Yue et al., Nat. Nanotechnol. 19, 1101 (2024)], the theoretical six-vertex model on the kagome lattice is systematically simulated using the directed loop Monte Carlo method. Four distinct vortex lattice phases are identified: (i) …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024407] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wei Zhang, Wanzhou Zhang, Jie Zhang, Chengxiang Ding, and Youjin Deng

Inspired by the experimental realization of direct kagome spin ice [Yue et al., Nat. Nanotechnol. 19, 1101 (2024)], the theoretical six-vertex model on the kagome lattice is systematically simulated using the directed loop Monte Carlo method. Four distinct vortex lattice phases are identified: (i) …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024407] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Phase transitions and critical exponents in the six-vertex model on kagome lattices

item.title: Optical control of a metastable phase in the charge density wave Mott insulator $1T\text{−}{\mathrm{TaS}}_{2}$ investigated using time- and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy
item.description:
Author(s): Tanusree Saha, Arindam Pramanik, Rokaya Osama, Fabio Frassetto, Damjan Krizmancic, Luca Poletto, Arun Ravindran, Barbara Ressel, Primož Rebernik Ribič, and Giovanni De Ninno

Light-induced metastable phases are exotic, long-lived out-of-equilibrium states of matter. Optical control offers a powerful approach to engineering these phases, enabling dynamic tuning of their electronic and structural properties. Using time- and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, we inv…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035113] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tanusree Saha, Arindam Pramanik, Rokaya Osama, Fabio Frassetto, Damjan Krizmancic, Luca Poletto, Arun Ravindran, Barbara Ressel, Primož Rebernik Ribič, and Giovanni De Ninno

Light-induced metastable phases are exotic, long-lived out-of-equilibrium states of matter. Optical control offers a powerful approach to engineering these phases, enabling dynamic tuning of their electronic and structural properties. Using time- and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, we inv…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035113] Published Tue Jan 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Optical control of a metastable phase in the charge density wave Mott insulator $1T\text{−}{\mathrm{TaS}}_{2}$ investigated using time- and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy
item.dc:creator: Tanusree Saha, Arindam Pramanik, Rokaya Osama, Fabio Frassetto, Damjan Krizmancic, Luca Poletto, Arun Ravindran, Barbara Ressel, Primož Rebernik Ribič, and Giovanni De Ninno
